response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced and technically broad developer, but his profile is misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position demands 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development as the primary language, whereas his C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project. His career has been dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. He has no evident experience with industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT, or industrial automation domains, which are central to the role's context and responsibilities.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency systems experience (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, concurrency) — transferable foundations even if in DLang rather than C++
Hands-on event-driven and distributed-systems architecture via GISCollective and microservices work, showing relevant architectural thinking
Strong software-craftsmanship and mentoring track record (TDD, code katas, workshops, open-source libraries) aligning with the mentoring and quality-mindset requirements
concerns: C++ proficiency is negligible for a role that lists it as the primary language: only ~6 months of documented C++ work over a 16+ year career
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, or any of the listed industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0), which are domain-critical
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices, industrial cybersecurity, or MQTT — all explicitly required or strongly advantageous competencies
